chapter_title.ar: بَابُ فَضْلِ الْحَجِّ وَ الْعُمْرَةِ وَ ثَوَابِهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 28 – Merits of the Hajj and the Umra, and their Rewards

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/126)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/126)
text: الْحَجُّ وَ الْعُمْرَةُ سُوقَانِ مِنْ أَسْوَاقِ الْآخِرَةِ اللَّازِمُ لَهُمَا فِي ضَمَانِ اللَّهِ إِنْ أَبْقَاهُ أَدَّاهُ إِلَى عِيَالِهِ وَ إِنْ أَمَاتَهُ أَدْخَلَهُ الْجَنَّةَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ali Bin Ismail, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Ja’far Bin Imran, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The Hajj and the Umra are two markets from the markets of the Hereafter. It is a pre-requisite for these two to be in the Guarantee of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> that if He<sup>azwj</sup> Keeps him alive, would Return him back to his family, and if He<sup>azwj</sup> Causes him to die, would Enter him into the Paradise’.
en.sarwar: 13. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ali ibn Isma‘il from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Ja’far ibn ‘Imran from abu Basir who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that al-Hajj and al-‘Umrah are two market places (that deal with the merchandise) of the next life. Those who attach themselves to these two are part of the undertaking of Allah. He returns them to their families if He decides to keep them living, or admits them in paradise if He decides to cause them to die.”
